rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RICHARD KWOCK ET AL VS. PAUL ZILIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 15, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT PAUL ZILIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. SFRO 37.9(a)(8) allows a landlord to recover possession to use the premises as his principal residence. For the purposes of SFRO 37.9(a)(8) the term landlord is defined as "a natural person¿ who hold[s] a recorded fee interest in the property." The person who purportedly is to move in is Mr. Kwock, an individual. The "person" who is attempting to recover possession for Mr. Kwock, an individual, to move in, is in the title owner of the property, a Trust. Mr. Kwock, an individual is not "a natural person¿ who hold[s] a recorded fee interest in the property." The Trust is not "a natural person" while it does "hold a recorded fee interest in the property." Mr. Kwock, as trustee, (holding legal title to property owed by a trust since the trust itself is simply a collection of assets and liabilities) is not the same "natural person" as Mr. Kwock, as individual, as a matter of law. In other words, Mr. Kwock, as individual, is not a landlord holding legal title; and the Trust, while acting via a "natural person," Mr. Kwock, as trustee, is not a "natural person" itself. Neither Mr. Kwock, as individual, not Mr. Kwock, as trustee, satisfy both requirements for the move-in per Reg. 12.14(a). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the two may be combined for the purposes of just cause eviction under SFRO. = (501/CFH)
